User Action: User connects phone or PC to Wi-Fi AP (SSID: ESP32-Oscilloscope). System powered by PCB 2 rails: 3.3 V LDO / 5 V LDO / −5 V rail.
Firmware / API: ESP32-S3 on PCB 1 hosts SoftAP + HTTP server on port 80
Display / Output: Browser loads full single-page web app from flash (INDEX_HTML ~50 KB)
Notes: PCB 1: DMM + ESP32 node | PCB 2: Power supply (fixed rails + programmable supply) | PCB 3: Waveform generator | PCB 4: Oscilloscope

Firmware / API: GET / served from PROGMEM; JS calls GET /control-state to restore waveform + supply settings
Display / Output: Oscilloscope starts polling at 20 fps; waveform and supply fields populate with last-known state
Notes: State is re-synced from device on every page load

User Action: Oscilloscope runs automatically; user selects window size (1 / 2 / 4 / 8 ms or custom)
Firmware / API: JS sends GET /adc/burst?n=N; firmware on PCB 1 bit-bangs SPI to AD7356 on PCB 4; collects N samples at 60 kHz with deadline scheduling (17 µs period)
Display / Output: Raw 12-bit ADC codes returned as calibrated voltages in JSON (chA / chB arrays)

User Action: Calibration applied automatically on every burst
Firmware / API: Piecewise linear fit: seg_0 slope=0.000997 intercept=−2.058 (raw<2817); seg_1 slope=0.000990 intercept=−2.038 (raw≥2817)
Display / Output: Voltages plotted on canvas with HiDPI DPR scaling; X-axis ticks in µs or ms
Notes: Fit derived from 3 known amplitude points (1.0 V / 1.5 V / 2.5 V Vpp); coefficients stored in cal_20260327_195422.txt

User Action: User adjusts Y-range (0.5–4 V preset or custom) and vertical center (±1 V preset or manual)
Firmware / API: No API call — pure client-side display state
Display / Output: Plot redraws with new voltage window; Range and Center status bar updates
Notes: Changing range/center does not re-fetch data

User Action: User enables trigger; selects rising or falling edge, channel (CH A or CH B), and level voltage
Firmware / API: When trigger on: JS requests 2× maxPoints samples for search headroom; findTriggerIndex() scans for first edge crossing
Display / Output: If crossing found: waveform frame snaps to trigger point. If not found: display holds last valid frame (no flicker)
Notes: Trigger level drawn as amber dashed line with voltage label on plot

User Action: User clicks Set Cursor A or B, then clicks or drags on plot (mouse or touch)
Firmware / API: No API call — cursor ratios stored client-side; interpolateSeries() reads voltage at cursor position
Display / Output: Cursor lines drawn in yellow (A) and cyan (B); Δt and ΔV computed and shown in Cursor Measurements panel
Notes: Mobile: touchend reads cursorPreview.ratio set during touchmove (event.touches is empty on finger lift)

User Action: Computed automatically after every burst; no user action
Firmware / API: computeChannelStats() runs on chA/chB slice: max / min / Vpp / RMS / zero-crossing frequency
Display / Output: Signal Measurements panel shows Vpp / RMS / Max / Min / Frequency for CH A and CH B
Notes: Frequency estimated by counting positive midpoint crossings over sample duration

User Action: User clicks Measure on the Resistance card
Firmware / API: GET /ads/measure?ch=0; ADS1115 reads AIN0 at 860 SPS for 500 ms (~430 oversampled); Python-calibrated inverse fit y = 40711274/x − 1522 applied, then piecewise error correction table
Display / Output: Resistance displayed in Ω or kΩ; raw ADC average shown; O.C. flag shown if bits ≤ 0.1
Notes: Python script generated 5-point inverse fit from DMM reference measurements: 9.8 Ω – 102 kΩ; R²=1.000; RMSE=9.9 Ω

User Action: User clicks Measure on the Current card
Firmware / API: GET /ads/measure?ch=1; AIN1 read; Python-calibrated linear fit y = −0.000654·x + 13.373 applied, then piecewise correction
Display / Output: Current displayed in A or mA; raw ADC average shown
Notes: Python script generated 6-point linear fit from reference current source: 10 mA – 499 mA; R²=0.9999; RMSE=1.3 mA

User Action: User clicks Measure on the DC Voltage card
Firmware / API: GET /ads/measure?ch=2; AIN2–3 differential read; Python-calibrated linear fit y = 0.000210·x + 0.000137 applied, then piecewise correction
Display / Output: Voltage displayed in V; raw ADC average shown
Notes: Python script generated 6-point linear fit from precision voltage reference: 1 mV – 5 V; R²=1.000; RMSE=0.3 mV

User Action: User types target voltage (0.640–3.420 V or 4.750–5.250 V) and clicks Set
Firmware / API: POST /power; firmware validates range, calls mic.setVoltage() → I2C VID code write to MIC24045 → reads back actual voltage + STATUS register
Display / Output: Supply card shows actual output voltage; status flags: Power Good / Overcurrent / Thermal Warning / Thermal Shutdown
Notes: PCB 2 also provides fixed 3.3 V LDO, 5 V LDO, and −5 V rail to all other boards. Gap 3.42–4.75 V is hardware-invalid and rejected.

User Action: User selects wave shape (Sine / Triangle / Square), enters frequency; page loads last state automatically
Firmware / API: POST /waveform; firmware clamps frequency (0.1–200000 Hz); calls wave.apply() → writes AD9833 frequency register via SPI (25 MHz MCLK)
Display / Output: Waveform status line confirms mode and frequency; AD9833 outputs signal immediately
Notes: Amplitude is fixed hardware output for current revision — amplitude calibration not yet implemented

Firmware / API: setup(): Serial → adcSampler.begin() + diagnose() → wavegen.begin() → I2C retry loop 2 s (MIC24045 + ADS1115) → startAccessPoint() → setupServerRoutes() → httpServer().begin()
Display / Output: Startup log buffered in RAM; type "log" in serial monitor to replay all init messages
Notes: diagnose() checks AD7356 SPI frame structure (leading/trailing bits), pin states, saturation, and burst sample rate
